description: In a busy coffee shop, Alice, Frank Jr., and Phoebe sit on an orange couch around a table. Frank Jr. sits between them, his right arm around Alice's shoulders. Phoebe holds Alice's left hand, looking at a ring on her finger. A voice faintly says, "You."
Phoebe keeps holding Alice's hand and looks at the ring, while Alice and Frank Jr. watch her.
"Oh, my God," Phoebe says, her mouth opening in a smile.
Phoebe shakes Alice's and Frank Jr.'s hands rapidly.
"I can't believe my little brother's married," Phoebe says, leaning forward and squeezing their hands as audience laughter plays.
Frank Jr. and Alice laugh. Phoebe leans in and embraces both of them at once. They separate, and Phoebe keeps her hands on their arms. Audience laughter continues.
"Why did you tell me you were eloping?" Phoebe asks as the laughter fades.
"Because it just sort of happened. You know, we were at the courthouse, we were having lunch," Frank Jr. replies, gesturing with his hands.
"Wait, why are we at the courthouse?" Alice asks, touching Frank Jr.'s arm. Frank Jr. turns to her and replies, "We were having lunch,"
Frank Jr. turns back toward Phoebe.

"...and then all of a sudden we were like, hey, you know, we're here, we're having lunch. Let's get married. Right?" Frank Jr. says, gesturing with both hands. He turns to Alice at the end of the sentence.
Alice nods and pats Frank Jr.'s hands.
"A year and a half ago, I didn't even know I had a brother. Now I have a sister, too," Phoebe says, leaning back slightly and smiling over audience laughter.
Phoebe leans forward and pulls Alice and Frank Jr. into another group hug. They separate, and Frank Jr. and Alice kiss each other on the lips. Phoebe watches them, smiling.
"Great," Phoebe says as they continue kissing.
Frank Jr. and Alice continue to kiss. Frank Jr. swings his right leg over Alice's lap, lying back onto her. Phoebe watches, hands in her lap, as audience laughter grows louder.
"Okay," Phoebe says, looking at them. Audience laughter continues.
Frank Jr. and Alice keep kissing as Frank Jr. fully lies across Alice's lap. Phoebe gestures toward them with both hands over ongoing audience laughter.
"Stop it," Phoebe says, prompting a peak in audience laughter.
"Don't," she adds, gesturing with her hands again.
Alice and Frank Jr. separate and sit up, Frank Jr. remaining mostly reclined against Alice.
"So, I'm going to get you a gift now. Is there anything you need?" Phoebe asks, spreading her hands open.
Frank Jr. sits up properly next to Alice.
"Yeah," Frank Jr. says, nodding.
Frank Jr. looks down, holding his hands together, then looks up at Phoebe.
"We've been trying to get pregnant pretty much ever since we got engaged," Alice says, holding Frank Jr.'s hand.
"Thought we'd get a jump on things. No one's getting any younger," Alice adds, gesturing with her free hand over brief audience laughter.
Phoebe smiles and nods at Alice.
"Because the thing is, we're not able to conceive," Frank Jr. says, looking at Phoebe.
Phoebe's smile fades as she listens.
"And we've tried everything. We've seen a bunch of doctors," Alice adds.
"Yeah. And they say that our only chance to have a baby is that if they take my sperm," Frank Jr. says, pointing to himself.
"...her egg, and put it together in a..." Frank Jr. continues, gesturing toward Alice and then bringing his hands together.
"...dish and then put it into another girl," Frank Jr. says, keeping his hands raised.
"So we were wondering if you could be the girl that we could put it into," Frank Jr. adds, pointing at Phoebe.
Phoebe stares at them, her mouth slightly open. She looks back and forth between Frank Jr. and Alice.
"It's a really nice gift," Phoebe says, her pitch lower and flatter than before, as audience laughter plays.
Phoebe looks slightly away, her expression unchanged.
"I was thinking of, like, a gravy boat," Phoebe adds. Audience laughter is heard.
Frank Jr. shifts his posture, resting his hand on his knee.
"We know it's a lot to ask. We don't have anyone else to go to," Alice says, holding her hands together.
"We could pay someone to do it, but we don't have that kind of..." Alice continues.
"...money. Plus your family," Frank Jr. says, rubbing his forehead with his hand.
Frank Jr. lowers his hand from his forehead.
"I don't know. I just don't want my baby to grow into some chick I don't know," Frank Jr. adds, gesturing with his hands over audience laughter.
Phoebe nods slowly, keeping her gaze on Frank Jr.
"Don't give us an answer yet. Think about it," Alice says.
"And if you say no, we will totally understand," Frank Jr. says, looking at Phoebe.
Phoebe nods.
"Okay," Phoebe says.
Phoebe picks up a yellow mug from the table. Frank Jr. touches Alice's arm.
"And also, we were wondering if you could give us a ride down to the train station," Frank Jr. says, pointing his thumb toward the door.
Phoebe holds the mug with both hands, looking at Frank Jr.
"Well, actually, we kind of need an answer to that one right now," Frank Jr. adds, followed by audience laughter.
Phoebe lowers the mug, still looking at Frank Jr. Background chatter is audible.
The scene changes to an apartment. Chandler sits on the floor by a coffee table holding playing cards. Monica sits across from him, and Rachel stands nearby holding cards.
A door clicks and swings open. Ross and Joey enter the apartment. Joey walks ahead, wearing a blue blazer over a red shirt.
"Hey, you guys, check it out. Check it out. Guess what job we just got," Joey says, smiling widely.
Chandler turns to look at Joey.
"I don't know, but Donald Trump wants his blue blazer black," Chandler says, holding his cards, prompting audience laughter.
Joey and Ross look at Chandler with still expressions.
"What?" Joey asks.
"Blue blazer back," Chandler says, emphasizing the words over audience laughter.
"He wants it back," Chandler adds, gesturing with his hand.
Rachel continues holding her cards.
"But you said black," Rachel says.
"Why would he want his blue blazer black?" Rachel asks.
Chandler looks at Rachel, his jaw tightening slightly.
"Well, you know what I meant," Chandler says, gesturing with his left hand.
"No. You messed it up," Monica says, turning toward Chandler.
Chandler looks away from Monica.
"You're stupid," Monica adds. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler keeps his gaze fixed on Monica, then raises a single card in his hand.
"So, what job did you get, Joe?" Chandler asks, lowering his card and looking at Joey.
"Oh, a tour guide at the museum. Yeah, Ross got it for me," Joey says, pointing his thumb at Ross.
"Well, how can you be a tour..." Rachel asks, stepping forward slightly.
"...guide? I mean, don't you have to be a dinosaur expert or something?" Rachel continues.
"No, not really. They give you all the information. It's like memorizing a script," Ross says, gesturing with his hands. "Yeah," Joey adds, nodding.
"And on your left you have Tyrannosaurus Rex, a carnivore from the..." Joey says, gesturing to his left.
Rachel looks at Joey, listening.
In an apartment kitchen, Joey and Ross stand facing each other near a counter.
Joey nods and says, "Very nice. Yeah."
Ross looks at Joey, maintaining his gaze.
Ross raises his hands slightly and says, "Actually, Joey, it's the Cretaceous Period."
Joey keeps his gaze on Ross in silence.
Joey smiles and says, "Yeah, but I can pronounce Jurassic."
The front door opens, and Phoebe enters wearing a tan coat and scarf, carrying a large bag.
Phoebe steps further inside and says, "Guess what?"

Phoebe walks toward the kitchen table, removing her coat. "Frank Jr. and Alice got married. Oh, my God. And they're gonna have a baby," she says. Monica, Chandler, and Rachel are visible in the living room area.
Phoebe holds her coat and a white piece of clothing, her mouth open in a wide smile.
Phoebe says, "And they want me to grow it for them in my uterus," holding the clothing in front of her.
Ross, Monica, Chandler, and Rachel stare at Phoebe in silence. Joey sits at the table nearby.
Monica steps forward and asks, "My God. Are you serious?"
Phoebe nods, saying, "Yeah," as she sets her coat down.
Joey, sitting at the table, turns to Phoebe and says, "You really thinking of having sex with your brother?"
Ross, Chandler, Monica, and Rachel turn to stare at Joey. Phoebe looks at him with her mouth open. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe grimaces and says, "Ew, and oh, no," gesturing with her hands.
Phoebe shakes her head and waves her hands dismissively.
Phoebe says, "They want me to be the surrogate."
Gesturing with her hands, Phoebe says, "It's her egg and his sperm."
Phoebe spreads her hands wide and says, "And I'm just the oven."
Lowering her hands, Phoebe says, "It's totally their bun."
Monica looks at Phoebe, her lips pressed together.
Monica asks, "What did you tell them?"
Phoebe gestures and says, "Well, they said that I had to think about it first. But what is there to think of? I'm going to be giving"
Phoebe continues, "someone the greatest gift you can possibly give," raising her hands again.
Chandler stands in the living room, facing Phoebe.
Chandler spreads his arms and says, "You're going to carry their child and get them a Sony PlayStation?"
Rachel steps toward Phoebe, closing the distance between them.
Rachel gestures and says, "Honey, this really is an incredible thing to do for them. But there are things to think about. Yeah, I mean, you'd be pregnant."
Rachel emphasizes her point, saying, "I mean, pregnant."
Phoebe replies, "I know," nodding her head.
Ross stands near Joey, looking at Phoebe.
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "Phoebes, you're talking about putting your body through an awful lot."
Ross continues, "I mean, morning sickness," holding up one hand.
Ross keeps his hand raised while maintaining his gaze on Phoebe.
Ross says, "labor and it's all for somebody else."
Phoebe looks at Ross and asks, "Yeah. What's your point?"
Ross looks at Joey momentarily, then turns back to Phoebe.
Ross points to himself and says, "Well, the stuff I just mentioned."
Rachel places a hand on her chest, looking at Phoebe.
Rachel says, "Wow, I don't know if I could ever do that. I always figured the first time I had a baby, it would be with someone I love. And that baby was"
Rachel continues, "a keeper," spreading her hands outward.
Phoebe looks at the group, her expression neutral.
Phoebe says, "You guys were a lot more supportive when I wanted to make denim furniture."
Chandler, now seated on the armrest of the couch next to Joey, leans forward.
Chandler says, "Listen, if you decide to do this, we'll be supportive like crazy. We just want you to think it through."
Rachel adds, "Yeah, honey, maybe you"
Rachel continues, "can talk to somebody who's had a baby like your mom."
Phoebe replies, "My mom never gave birth," tilting her head slightly.
Phoebe opens her mouth slightly and raises her index finger.
Pointing toward the group, Phoebe says, "But my birth mom did."
The setting changes to the exterior of Central Perk coffee shop, where snow is visible on the ground. Inside, Chandler and a woman in a red coat stand near the door.
Chandler and the woman embrace and kiss, their arms wrapped around each other.
The woman pulls back slightly, her hands near his waist, and says, "I love this tushy."
The woman asks, "Take it to work with me?"
Chandler replies, "Yeah, sure. It's not mine anyway. Came with the pants." The woman says, "Oh," and smiles.
The woman turns and exits through the door.
An uncertain speaker says, "Truth?" as Chandler turns toward the seating area.
Chandler walks toward the couch where Rachel and Monica are seated.
Rachel looks at Chandler and says, "I am so jealous. You guys are really just right there, aren't you?"
Chandler stops walking, puts his hands in his pockets, and replies, "Yes."
Chandler stands facing Rachel and Monica.
Monica looks at Chandler and asks, "Right where?"
Monica continues looking at him, waiting for an answer.
Monica says, "Beginning where it's all sex and talking and sex and talking."
Chandler steps forward slightly, shifting his posture.
Chandler smiles and says, "Yeah, you got to love the talking."
Monica keeps her gaze on Chandler.
Monica says, "And the sex."
Chandler raises his hands defensively and says, "All right, we haven't had sex yet, okay? But what's the big deal? This is special, and I want our love to grow"
Chandler lowers his hands and continues, "before we move on to the next level."
Rachel and Monica stare at him in silence.
Rachel says, "Chandler, that is so nice."
Ross, seated in an armchair nearby holding a blue mug, says, "That is really nice lying."
Chandler turns his head to look at Ross.
In a coffee shop, Rachel, Monica, Chandler, and Ross sit around a coffee table. Chandler, sitting on the arm of the sofa, says, "No way. Is that the reason."
Monica, seated on the sofa next to Rachel, turns to Chandler and asks, "Why? Just because you're not mature enough to understand something like that?"
Chandler leans forward, standing up slightly, and says, "No, he's right. I'm totally lying," finishing his line as he sits back down on the sofa arm.
Ross smiles. Rachel flips her hair while Monica looks at Chandler, resting her chin on her hand.
Monica asks, "Then what is it?" Chandler leans back, spreading his arms along the sofa back, and says, "Well, Kathy's last boyfriend was Joey."
Ross, holding a blue mug, asks, "And you're afraid you won't be able..."
Ross finishes, "...to fill his shoes?"
Chandler stares at Ross, his face motionless.
Chandler says, "No, I'm afraid I won't be able to make love as well as him," his hands resting in his lap.
Chandler remains seated with his hands in his lap.
Ross gestures with one hand and says, "Yeah, I was going for the metaphor."
Chandler gestures toward Ross and replies, "Yes, and I was saying the actual words."
Ross smiles and nods.
Rachel says, "Well, big deal. So Joey's had a lot of girlfriends. It doesn't mean that he's great in bed," lightly shrugging her shoulders.
Chandler points to his chest and says, "We share a wall."
Rachel raises her hands, palms facing forward. Monica shifts her gaze from Rachel to Chandler.
Chandler gestures with his hands as he says, "So either he's great in bed or she just liked to agree with him a lot."
Rachel twists a strand of hair while Monica looks at Chandler, nodding slightly.
Chandler stares forward and says, "Loudly and a lot. All night long. She'd agree," keeping his arms spread on the sofa.
Ross stares at Chandler, motionless. Rachel and Monica cross their arms.
Monica leans forward and says, "Sweetie, with you it's going to be different. The sex is going to be great because you guys are in love."
Chandler looks toward Monica, his hands clasped.
Ross says, "Yeah, just go for it, Chandler."
Rachel adds, "Yeah, you should."
Chandler stands up and says, "Really? All right. I'll sleep with my girlfriend."
Chandler walks past the coffee table toward the exit.
Chandler stops, points back toward the group, and says, "But I'm just doing it for you guys," before turning away.
Chandler walks out of the coffee shop. The scene changes to a museum hallway where Joey, wearing a blue blazer, stands before a group of children near a large skull exhibit.
Joey stops near the display, holding up a hand, and says to the kids, "Okay. Now, the mastodon is from the semi late Jurassic period?"
The Smart Kid, wearing a red sweater, raises a finger.
The Smart Kid asks, "Is it the mastodon from the Pleistocene..."
The Smart Kid adds, "...Epoch."
Joey leans toward the boy and says, "This is a museum. No talking."
Joey straightens up and points ahead, guiding the children down the hall.
Joey stops at another display and says, "Right down here we have..."
Joey points to a skeletal foot on a pedestal and finishes, "...a large foot."
Joey nods at the display, and the children look at it. He then gestures further down the hall.
Pointing through an observation window at Ross, who wears a lab coat inside an exhibit, Joey says, "And over here we have Ross Geller."
Joey knocks on the glass and says, "Everyone wave hi to Ross."
As Ross turns and waves back, Joey continues, "Ross is one of our most important scientists. Look at him hard at work."
Ross points to his temple, mouths a word, points a tool toward the group, and smiles.
Joey says, "Okay, moving right along," as he herds the children away from the window.
The scene changes to the exterior of a coastal house. Inside, Phoebe Sr. works on a clay sculpture at a table.
A knock is heard. Phoebe Sr. says, "It's open. Come in." Phoebe enters the kitchen and says, "Hi. I'm sorry I'm late." Phoebe Sr. replies, "Oh, that's okay."
Wiping her hands on a cloth, Phoebe Sr. says, "It gave me time to finish glazing my nipples."
Phoebe takes off her coat and says, "Wow, really go all out when you're expecting company."
Phoebe Sr. crosses her arms and says, "Now, I was working on my pottery." Phoebe turns, looks at the table, and says, "Oh,"
Phoebe points toward the table.
Pointing toward the table, Phoebe says, "I didn't know that you did pot."
Phoebe Sr. looks at Phoebe.
Phoebe Sr. says, "Well, yeah, mostly nudes. It combines my two passions: pottery and erotica."
Phoebe smiles, points forward, and says, "Oh, erotery, hey." The two women embrace.
The two women separate from the hug.
Phoebe Sr. says, "Well, thanks for coming out to see me. I just thought it would be a very good idea to talk about this baby stuff in person. You know,"
Phoebe stands facing Phoebe Sr., her arms resting at her sides.
Phoebe Sr. looks down, then meets Phoebe's gaze, saying, "I really don't think it's a very good idea, Phoebe."
Phoebe asks, "Why not?"
Phoebe Sr. says, "Well, because you'd be giving up a baby," gesturing with one hand.
Phoebe Sr. adds, "And"
Phoebe looks at her, motionless.
Phoebe Sr. continues, "I don't know if there's anything that I could say that could make you understand the pain of giving up a baby,"
Phoebe Sr. pauses.
Phoebe Sr. adds, "so," turning away to bend over a cardboard box on the floor.
Phoebe Sr. lifts a small pug puppy out of the box.
Phoebe reacts, stepping back and holding up her hands, speaking faster than her previous lines as she says, "Oh, no, I understand the pain. Don't, don't hurt the puppy."
Phoebe Sr. holds the puppy toward Phoebe and says, "No, the puppy is yours." Phoebe's eyes widen as she takes the puppy, saying, "Oh, I get a puppy?"
Phoebe Sr. says, "Well, yeah. I mean, yeah, but for only three days."
Phoebe, holding the puppy to her chest, asks, "Why?"
In a living room, Phoebe sits holding a small pug puppy, keeping her gaze on it. Phoebe Sr. stands nearby with her arms folded, looking at Phoebe.
Phoebe Sr. says, "I realize that I don't have any right to start getting all parenty on you and everything now. But," gesturing slightly with one hand. Phoebe continues to stroke the puppy.
Phoebe keeps her head lowered, stroking the puppy's head. Phoebe Sr. watches her, her arms remaining folded.
Phoebe Sr. says, "Phoebe, would you please look at me and not the puppy." Phoebe's gaze remains on the puppy.
"It's very important," Phoebe Sr. continues. Phoebe raises her head to look at her.
Phoebe keeps her gaze on Phoebe Sr.
Phoebe Sr. says, "Okay. I mean, I know what I'm talking about. I gave up two babies." Phoebe looks at her, then lowers her gaze back to the puppy.

Phoebe Sr. says, "And I only wish that I had someone there who had given up babies that could have told me how terrible it is to give up babies," unfolding her arms and gesturing. Phoebe continues to stroke the puppy.
Phoebe Sr. looks at Phoebe. Phoebe continues looking at the puppy.
Phoebe Sr. says, "I really shouldn't have given you the puppy first," gesturing with both hands. Phoebe looks up at her.
Phoebe lowers her head close to the puppy.
Phoebe says, "That's okay," her face near the puppy.
Phoebe adds, "Now we're listening," her face still near the puppy.
Phoebe Sr. says, "I just think that it would be something that you would regret every single day for the rest of your life." Phoebe looks at her, then back down at the puppy.
Phoebe strokes the puppy. Phoebe Sr. reaches out and touches the puppy's fur.
Phoebe Sr. says, "So however hard it is for you to give up this puppy it would be like a million times harder to give up a child," gesturing with her hands. Phoebe looks at her.
Phoebe looks down, then back up to Phoebe Sr.
Phoebe says, "Okay."
"Fair enough. Three days," Phoebe continues, nodding slightly.
A bell rings loudly. Phoebe Sr. looks toward the sound.
Phoebe Sr. says, "It's time to take my ass out of the kiln," turning and walking out of the frame.
The setting changes to a cafeteria where Joey sits at a table with other men wearing blue blazers. A Tour Guide in a similar uniform walks by holding a tray.
Joey turns in his seat toward the Tour Guide as she approaches.
Joey gestures to an empty seat and asks, "Would you mind sitting there? I'm saving this seat for my friend Ross." The Tour Guide stops and asks, "You mean Dr. Geller?"
"Doctor? Oh, I didn't know he had a nickname," Joey says, smiling slightly.
The Tour Guide looks at Joey.
The Tour Guide says, "Oh, he won't sit here."
The Tour Guide says, "Only the people in the white coats sit over there. And only the people in the blue blazers sit over here," pointing across the room and then toward Joey's table.
Joey looks at her.
"How come?" Joey asks. "That's just the way it is," the Tour Guide replies. "That's crazy," Joey says. The Tour Guide responds, "Maybe it's crazy in a perfect world, a world without lab"
"coats and blazers," the Tour Guide completes her sentence.
The Tour Guide points her fork toward Joey.
"But you're not in a perfect world. You're in a museum now," the Tour Guide continues.
The Tour Guide turns her head to look across the cafeteria.
"See that scientist with glasses? He and I used to play together all the time in grade school," the Tour Guide says, pointing. Across the room, a man in a white coat and glasses sits at a table.
"But now Peter," the Tour Guide says, looking toward the man.
The Tour Guide leans forward slightly.
"Hey, Peter," the Tour Guide shouts toward him, her volume increasing.
"It's me Rhonda from P.S. 129," the Tour Guide continues shouting loudly. Peter continues eating, not looking up.
The Tour Guide maintains her gaze on Peter.
"I shared my pudding with you, man," the Tour Guide shouts.
Peter continues eating without responding.
"I gave you my Snack Pack," the Tour Guide shouts, her volume remaining high.
The Tour Guide turns back to look at Joey.
"See, he pretends he don't even hear me," the Tour Guide says to Joey, her volume dropping to a conversational level.
Joey looks at her, his expression remaining still.
"I think everybody's pretending they don't hear you," Joey says to the Tour Guide.
The Tour Guide smiles slightly.
"Anyway, look, I don't know about you and your jackets and your separate tables. But Ross is one of my best friends," Joey says, gesturing with his hands.
Joey continues gesturing with his hands while looking at the Tour Guide.
The Tour Guide looks at Joey as he holds his hands open.
